title: Butter Chicken
excerpt: Ingredients 1 kg chicken breast fillets, cut into 2cm cubes or strips juice of one lemon or lime 6 whole cloves 12 whole peppercorns large, whole cinnamon stick, broken into smaller pieces 2 bay leaves 15 ml whole coriander seeds 10 ml whole cumin seeds seeds of 6 – …
html:
Ingredients

  • 1 kg chicken breast fillets, cut into 2cm cubes or strips
  • juice of one lemon or lime
  • 6 whole cloves
  • 12 whole peppercorns
  • large, whole cinnamon stick, broken into smaller pieces
  • 2 bay leaves
  • 15 ml whole coriander seeds
  • 10 ml whole cumin seeds
  • seeds of 6 – 8 cardamom pods
  • 12 whole almonds
  • 5 – 10 ml dried chilli flakes (or to taste)
  • 10 ml turmeric
  • 30 ml sunflower oil
  • 2 large onions, finely chopped
  • 30 ml fresh garlic, finely chopped (or to taste)
  • 30 ml fresh ginger, finely grated
  • 1 x 400 g tin tomato puree
  • 500 ml chicken stock
  • a handful of curry leaves
  • 250 ml plain full-cream yoghurt
  • salt and white pepper to taste
  • 90 ml butter
  • a big handful of chopped fresh coriander leaves

Method

Mix the chicken in a plastic or glass bowl with the lemon juice, cover the bowl and set aside.

Toast all the spices, almonds and chilli flakes in a hot pan until fragrant and process in a coffee grinder or with a mortar and pestle to a fine powder. Set aside.

Heat the oil in a pot and sauté the onions in it until golden brown. Add the garlic and ginger. Fry lightly until fragrant and add the spice mixture, tomato puree, chicken stock and curry leaves. As soon as it comes to the boil,
lower the heat and let it simmer without a lid until it has thickened well.

Now melt half the butter in a large pan, sauté the chicken lightly in it and add it to the sauce along with the yoghurt. Season to taste, stir everything through and cook for a few minutes just until the chicken
is cooked through, no longer. Stir in the remaining butter, garnish with coriander leaves and serve.

Makes 6 generous portions.

A bit of work, but easy!
